title: RealReal (REAL) Q3 2025 Earnings Show a 17% Revenue Rise
text: The RealReal's third-quarter revenues increased 17 percent to $174 million, producing a gross merchandise volume of $520 million. Third-quarter adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose to $9.3 million from $2.3 million a year earlier. Adjusted losses per share narrowed to 4 cents from 9 cents in the third quarter last year, better than the 6 cent deficit analysts projected. Over the past year the company's annual active buyer count rose by 7 percent to 1 million. The company also achieved strong growth in direct revenue, with direct revenue growing 47% year-over-year in the third quarter. The RealReal is looking for revenues to hit $687 million to $690 million this year, above the $675 million analysts forecast and the $667 million to $674 million the firm previously anticipated in August. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA is seen hitting $37.7 million to $38.7 million, up from the $29 million to $32 million it previously guided investors to. Athena, their AI-enabled product intake process, is a key driver of operational efficiencies. It processed 27% of items by the end of Q3 and is expected to touch 30% to 40% by year-end. Athena is projected to save a couple of dollars per item, contributing to ongoing productivity improvements. Investors appreciated the progress and traded shares of the company up 7.1 percent to $12 in after-hours trading on Wall Street.
